title: Cohere Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Cohere** is a multilingual LLM-as-a-service provider exposing a REST API suite under `https://api.cohere.com`. This connector — `CohereConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "cohere"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`) — wraps the inference + model-management surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Chat | `/v2/chat` | Multi-turn chat completions (Command R, Command R+, Command A) |
| Embed | `/v2/embed` | Vector embeddings (Embed v3 / v4) for search/RAG |
| Rerank | `/v2/rerank` | Cross-encoder relevance reranking (Rerank v3) |
| Classify | `/v1/classify` | Few-shot text classification |
| Tokenize / Detokenize | `/v1/tokenize`, `/v1/detokenize` | Per-model tokenisation |
| Models | `/v1/models` | List + describe available models |
| Datasets | `/v1/datasets` | List + register fine-tune datasets |
| Connectors | `/v1/connectors` | Cohere RAG connectors (provider-managed) |
| Fine-tuning | `/v1/finetuning/finetuned-models` | List + inspect fine-tune jobs |

The connector surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), retries 429/5xx with exponential backoff (3 attempts, capped at 16 s), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::CohereHTTPClient`). Cohere is an inference API — `sync()` is a no-op returning `SyncResult.SUCCESS` with 0 documents.

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Generate a Cohere API key

1. Sign in to https://dashboard.cohere.com.
2. Open **API Keys** in the left nav.
3. Click **Create Production Key** (Trial keys are throttled to 5 RPM and not suitable for live tenants).
4. Copy the key — Cohere shows it only once.

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Cohere**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `api_key` (secret) | API key from step 3 |
| `base_url` (string, optional) | Override `https://api.cohere.com` (rarely needed) |
| `default_chat_model` (string, optional) | Default `command-r-plus`. Override to `command-a-03-2025` for the larger model. |
| `default_embed_model` (string, optional) | Default `embed-v4.0`. |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number, optional) | Soft cap, default 100 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates the key by calling `GET /v1/models?page_size=1`, persists config, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, CONNECTED)`.

### 3. First health check

The gateway calls `health_check()` which lists 1 model as a lightweight probe. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401/403 → `OFFLINE + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Cohere REST API uses **server-to-server Bearer token authentication** (`AUTH_TYPE = "api_key"`). No OAuth dance, no token refresh, no expiry.

### Credential model

- `api_key` is sent in the `Authorization` header as `Bearer <key>` (note: unlike Wix, Cohere REQUIRES the `Bearer ` prefix).
- `X-Client-Name: shielva-connector` is sent on every request so Cohere's per-client analytics attribute platform traffic correctly.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `api_key`. Calls `GET /v1/models?page_size=1` so a bad key is caught immediately. 401 → `INVALID_CREDENTIALS + OFFLINE`. |
| `authorize(...)` | Returns a `TokenInfo` whose `access_token` is the api_key and `token_type` is `"api_key"`. No real token exchange. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /v1/models?page_size=1` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` | Not called — no token to refresh. |
| token storage | None — the api_key lives in `self.config`. |

### Header contract

Every outbound request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <api_key>
Content-Type:  application/json
Accept:        application/json
X-Client-Name: shielva-connector
```

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `api_key` | secret | yes | `self.api_key` | `Authorization: Bearer <key>` |
| `base_url` | string | no | `self.base_url` (defaults to `https://api.cohere.com`) | Override for sandbox/proxy |
| `default_chat_model` | string | no | `self.default_chat_model` (default `command-r-plus`) | Used when caller omits the model arg to `chat()` |
| `default_embed_model` | string | no | `self.default_embed_model` (default `embed-v4.0`) | Used when caller omits the model arg to `embed()` |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 100) | Client-side soft cap (informational) |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["api_key"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("OFFLINE",   "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_COHERE_BASE        = "https://api.cohere.com"
_DEFAULT_CHAT_MODEL = "command-r-plus"
_DEFAULT_EMBED_MODEL = "embed-v4.0"
```

The http client auto-strips a trailing `/v1` or `/v2` from `base_url`, so pasting either version of the API base URL is safe.

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `CohereConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::CohereHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `api_key`; call `GET /v1/models?page_size=1` to verify; persist config.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
No-op for api_key auth — returns a TokenInfo wrapping the api_key.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Lists 1 model via `GET /v1/models?page_size=1`. Classifies failures by `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
No-op (`SyncResult.SUCCESS`, 0 documents). Cohere is inference-only — there is no corpus to ingest.

children.title: Inference (v2)
children.content:
### `async chat(model, messages, *, temperature=0.3, max_tokens=1024, stream=False, p=None, k=None, stop_sequences=None) -> Dict`
`POST /v2/chat` — body: `{model, messages, temperature, max_tokens, stream, [p, k, stop_sequences]}`. Returns the v2 chat response with `message.content[{type: text, text: ...}]`.

### `async embed(model, texts, *, input_type="search_document", embedding_types=["float"], truncate="END") -> Dict`
`POST /v2/embed` — body: `{model, texts, input_type, embedding_types, truncate}`. Use `input_type="search_query"` for queries and `search_document` for indexed text.

### `async rerank(model, query, documents, *, top_n=10, return_documents=False) -> Dict`
`POST /v2/rerank` — body: `{model, query, documents, top_n, return_documents}`.

children.title: Inference utilities (v1)
children.content:
### `async classify(model, inputs, examples) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/classify` — body: `{model, inputs, examples}`.

### `async tokenize(model, text) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/tokenize` — body: `{model, text}`.

### `async detokenize(model, tokens) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/detokenize` — body: `{model, tokens}`.

children.title: Models, Datasets, Fine-tuning
children.content:
### `async list_models(*, page_size=20, page_token=None, endpoint=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/models?page_size={N}&page_token={C}&endpoint={chat|embed|rerank|classify}`.

### `async get_model(model_id) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/models/{model_id}`.

### `async list_datasets(*, dataset_type=None, limit=50) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/datasets`.

### `async create_dataset(payload) -> Dict`
`POST /v1/datasets` — JSON envelope only (file upload is multipart and shaped by the gateway).

### `async list_connectors(*, page_size=20, page_token=None) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/connectors` — Cohere RAG connectors, not Shielva connectors.

### `async list_finetuned_models(*, page_size=20) -> Dict`
`GET /v1/finetuning/finetuned-models`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
CohereError                              # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── CohereAuthError                      # 401 / 403
├── CohereBadRequestError                # 400 / 422
├── CohereNotFoundError                  # 404
├── CohereRateLimitError                 # 429 — retry_after_s
└── CohereServerError                    # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
CohereNetworkError = CohereServerError   # legacy name from older code
CohereNotFound     = CohereNotFoundError
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 401 / 403 / 404 / 422 | Raise immediately |
| 429 | Exponential backoff `_BACKOFF_BASE * 2 ** attempt + jitter`, capped at 16 s, retry up to `_max_retries=3` |
| 5xx | Same exponential backoff, retry up to 3 |
| `httpx.TimeoutException` / `httpx.NetworkError` | Backoff, retry up to 3, then raise `CohereNetworkError` |

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 / 403 → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE / UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429       → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)   — logged as warning
network   → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, CONNECTED)
other     → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `api_key` blank | Re-enter the key in ACP. Cohere shows the key only once at generation — rotate if lost. |
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at install | 401 — key revoked or typo | Verify the key in Cohere Dashboard → API Keys. Trial keys cap at 5 RPM and may appear to fail under load — promote to Production. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 — key lacks scope | Production keys grant all endpoints. Trial keys cannot call fine-tuning APIs — promote to Production. |
| `cohere.http.retry` warnings spike | Hitting 429 (per-minute rate cap) | The client retries with exponential backoff. Lower request rate or upgrade tier. Trial = 5 RPM, Production = thousands. |
| 404 on `get_model(model_id)` | Wrong model name (e.g. `command-r+` vs `command-r-plus`) | Call `list_models()` and use the exact `name` value. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'CohereNetworkError'` | Older exceptions.py without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `CohereNetworkError = CohereServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| `chat()` returns empty `message.content` | Caller used v1 chat shape with `message`-string | v2 chat expects `messages=[{role, content}]` not the v1 `message=str` shape. Migrate to the v2 envelope. |
